title: Housing
summary: Find a realistic place to stay and understand rental basics before arrival.
body: Housing is the most time-sensitive part of settling in. Start early, check whether your university has reserved rooms or vetted partners, and be careful with deposits, identity documents, and contracts.

title: Visa and residence
summary: Understand whether you need an MVV, residence permit, biometrics, or permit pickup.
body: Immigration steps depend on nationality and study programme. Your university is often the recognised sponsor for study permits, so follow its deadlines and keep IND messages together.

title: Documents
summary: Prepare identification, admission, housing, insurance, and legalised documents.
body: Documents are easier to fix before departure than after arrival. Make a digital and paper folder and check whether any civil documents need legalisation, apostille, or translation.

title: Municipality, BSN, and DigiD
summary: Register with the municipality, receive your BSN, and set up DigiD.
body: For longer stays, municipality registration enters you in the BRP and gives you a BSN. After that, DigiD becomes important for online government, healthcare, and education services.

title: Money and banking
summary: Plan access to money, understand IBANs, and arrange payment methods.
body: A Dutch or European bank account can make rent, salary, refunds, and subscriptions easier. Keep backup payment options until your local setup works.

title: Healthcare and GP
summary: Choose the right insurance and know how to access non-urgent care.
body: Health insurance rules depend on whether you only study, work, or do an internship. For non-urgent care, register with a GP near your address once you know where you live.

title: Transport and daily life
summary: Set up practical mobility, phone access, and daily-life basics.
body: Public transport, biking, phone access, and backup payment options make the first weeks easier. Check routes before appointments and budget for bike essentials if you plan to cycle.
